sections.heading: Architecture Overview
sections.content:
The viz dashboard is a single-page application served by a FastAPI backend.

```
cortex/viz/
  server.py          # FastAPI app, 36 API endpoints, serves static files
  static/
    index.html       # Single HTML shell with view containers
    css/style.css    # Custom styles + Cytoscape overrides
    js/
      app.js         # Main controller: state, init, filters, orchestration, project switcher
      graph.js       # Cytoscape.js graph renderer (344 lines)
      list.js        # Sortable node table (84 lines)
      detail.js      # Node detail drawer (313 lines)
      docs.js        # DocJSON viewer/editor (2505 lines)
      tests.js       # Test explorer with JUnit (890 lines)
      workflow.js    # Workflow/task step explorer (682 lines)
```

**Startup:** `cortex viz .` calls `run_server(project_root)` which calls `_apply_project()` to set module globals, `_upsert_registry()` to auto-register the project in `~/.cortex/projects.json`, then launches uvicorn. The `--no-browser` flag suppresses auto-open.

**Project switching:** The server supports hot-swapping the active project via `POST /api/projects/switch`. A `threading.Lock` (`_switch_lock`) guards the global reassignment. The frontend's `_loadProject()` method is re-callable — on switch it refetches `/api/meta` and `/api/all` and re-renders the full UI. Event bindings in `init()` are guarded by `_initialized` to avoid duplicate listeners.

**State management:** `App.state` is a single JS object holding the current view mode, all nodes/edges, staleness map, filter settings, focus mode, and dFlow integration data. All modules (`Graph`, `List`, `Detail`, `Docs`, `Tests`, `Workflow`) read from and write to `App.state`.

sections.heading: Sequence of Operations
sections.content:
### Page Load
```
browser GET / → index.html
browser loads app.js, graph.js, list.js, detail.js, docs.js, tests.js, workflow.js
App.init():
  1. Wire event handlers (view buttons, filters, depth slider, focus mode)
  2. GET /api/meta → project name, node/edge/doc counts
  3. GET /api/all → full node + edge lists + staleness + verification maps
  4. Apply sidebar filters → filteredNodes
  5. Graph.render(filteredNodes, edges) → Cytoscape init
```

### Node Selection
```
User clicks node in graph or list
  App.selectNode(id):
    1. GET /api/nodes/{id} → full node detail
    2. GET /api/nodes/{id}/history → change log
    3. GET /api/nodes/{id}/tests → test results
    4. (If workflow/test) GET /api/workflow/{id}/steps or /api/test/{id}/steps
    5. Detail.show(node, history, staleness, steps, tests)
```

### Rescan
```
User clicks Rescan button
  POST /api/rescan → server runs cortex build . synchronously
  GET /api/all → refresh everything
```

sections.heading: Data Model
sections.content:
The viz server reads directly from `cortex.db`. Key queries:

| Endpoint | Tables | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/api/all` | nodes, edges, tags, node_verification | Full graph payload |
| `/api/check` | nodes (re-parses source files) | Staleness computation |
| `/api/nodes/{id}` | nodes, tags | Single node detail |
| `/api/nodes/{id}/neighborhood` | edges, nodes | Ego-network graph |
| `/api/nodes/{id}/history` | node_history | Change log |
| `/api/nodes/{id}/tests` | test_results, test_runs | Test coverage |
| `/api/workflows` | nodes (dflow_meta filter) | dFlow-decorated workflows |
| `/api/tests` | nodes (dflow_meta filter) | dFlow-decorated tests |
| `/api/fixtures` | edges (validates), nodes | Fixture→test mapping |
| `/api/junit` | test_results, test_runs | Latest JUnit results |
| `/api/docs/*` | docs, doc_sections (+ filesystem) | DocJSON CRUD |

Staleness is computed on-the-fly by `_compute_staleness_rich()` — it re-reads source files and compares hashes against the database.

sections.heading: Implementation Trade-offs
sections.content:
### Vanilla JS, no framework
The frontend uses no React/Vue/Svelte. This keeps the dependency footprint to zero for the JS side. Cytoscape.js, Monaco, and Mermaid are loaded from CDN. The trade-off is more boilerplate DOM manipulation.

### Full graph payload on load
`/api/all` sends every node and edge in one response. For large projects (>5000 nodes), `App.state.isLargeProject` is set and the graph view starts in list mode. This avoids slow Cytoscape rendering.

### Staleness computed on-the-fly (not cached)
`/api/check` re-reads source files every time. This guarantees freshness but can be slow on very large codebases. The UI caches the staleness map in `App.state.stalenessMap` after each check.

### DocJSON editing in-browser
Docs.js (2505 lines) is the largest module because it implements a full inline editor: section editing, heading editing, link picker, tag management, Mermaid diagram editor, folder tree navigation. This keeps the doc-editing workflow inside the dashboard.

sections.heading: Technical Verification
sections.content:
- Graph renders with correct node colors per type and staleness borders
- Filter sidebar correctly excludes/includes node types and tags
- Focus mode shows correct ego-network at specified depth and direction
- Detail panel displays all node fields: ID, location, summary, docstring, steps, history, tests
- Rescan button triggers rebuild and refreshes all views
- DocJSON editor saves changes back to disk via PUT /api/docs/{id}
- Large project mode (>5000 nodes) defaults to list view
- Monaco source viewer loads correct file and highlights correct line
